title: Video Streaming Platform
prompt: Design a video streaming platform like YouTube or Netflix. Users should be able to upload videos, which are transcoded into multiple formats and resolutions. Viewers should be able to stream videos with adaptive bitrate. Optionally support live streaming.

functional: Transcode videos into multiple resolutions (240p, 480p, 720p, 1080p, 4K) and codecs (H.264, H.265, VP9)
Adaptive bitrate streaming (switch quality based on network conditions)
Video search and discovery (title, tags, recommendations)
Optional: live streaming with low latency
nonFunctional: Support global distribution with low latency worldwide
Transcoding should complete within minutes for typical videos
Cost-efficient storage for petabyte-scale video data

scale.storage: 500 PB total video storage, 500 hours of video uploaded per minute

keyQuestions: What video formats and resolutions do we need to support?
Do we need live streaming or only video-on-demand?
What is the maximum upload size?
What regions need to be served with low latency?

keyCalculations: Upload rate: 500 hrs/min * 60 = 30K hours of video/hour
Storage per video: 1 hour raw = ~10 GB, transcoded to 5 resolutions = ~15 GB total
Daily upload storage: 500 hrs/min * 1440 min * 15 GB = ~10 PB/day
Streaming bandwidth: 1M concurrent * 5 Mbps avg = 5 Tbps
Transcode compute: 500 hrs/min of video, each needing ~2x real-time = 1000 CPU-hours/min
CDN cache: top 10% of videos serve 90% of views => cache ~50 PB at edge

expectedEndpoints: POST /api/v1/videos/upload/init { title, description, tags } => { uploadId, uploadUrl }
GET /api/v1/videos/{videoId}/manifest.m3u8 => HLS manifest with quality variants

expectedEntities: videos: id, uploader_id, title, description, tags[], status (processing/ready/failed), duration, upload_time
video_variants: id, video_id, resolution, codec, bitrate, storage_url, segment_count
segments: video_variant_id, segment_number, duration, size, storage_url
interactions: id, user_id, video_id, type (like/dislike/comment), content, timestamp
view_events: video_id, user_id, timestamp, watch_duration, quality, region

expectedComponents: Upload Service (chunked upload to object storage)
Transcoding Pipeline (distributed job queue with GPU workers)
Object Storage (S3-like for raw and transcoded video segments)
Streaming Service (HLS/DASH manifest generation, segment serving)
CDN (global edge network for video segment delivery)
Analytics Pipeline (view counts, watch time aggregation)

suggestedTopics: Transcoding pipeline: DAG-based job orchestration, parallel segment transcoding, priority queues
Adaptive bitrate streaming: HLS vs DASH, segment duration trade-offs, player-side ABR algorithms
CDN architecture: multi-tier caching (edge -> regional -> origin), cache warming for viral content
Live streaming: RTMP ingest, real-time transcoding, HLS low-latency extensions, edge push
Cost optimization: storage tiering (hot/warm/cold), just-in-time transcoding for long-tail content
Content delivery: pre-fetching next segments, buffering strategy

checklist: Asked about supported formats and resolutions
Estimated upload volume and storage growth

anchors.1: Skipped requirements, jumped to architecture without understanding video-specific needs
anchors.2: Asked about basic upload/playback but missed transcoding, CDN, or scale requirements
anchors.3: Covered upload, transcoding, streaming, and CDN requirements with scale estimates
anchors.4: Proactively identified live streaming latency constraints, codec trade-offs, DRM needs, and cost implications of storage growth

checklist: Used object storage for video data

anchors.1: Single server serving raw uploaded files with no transcoding or CDN
anchors.2: Basic upload + transcode + serve but no CDN, no adaptive streaming
anchors.3: Upload pipeline, distributed transcoding, CDN delivery, adaptive bitrate with HLS/DASH
anchors.4: Multi-tier CDN, DAG-based transcoding orchestration, segment-level parallelism, and clear separation of hot/cold paths

checklist: Addressed CDN caching and cache warming strategies
Considered live streaming architecture if applicable

anchors.1: No depth on transcoding, streaming protocol, or CDN strategy
anchors.2: Basic discussion of one component but no concrete implementation details
anchors.3: Deep dive into transcoding pipeline + CDN strategy or ABR streaming with trade-offs
anchors.4: Thorough analysis of segment-parallel transcoding, HLS low-latency extensions, multi-tier CDN with quantitative cache analysis

checklist: Eager transcoding (all formats upfront) vs lazy transcoding (on-demand)
Segment duration: shorter (lower latency) vs longer (better compression)
H.264 vs H.265 vs VP9 codec trade-offs
CDN push vs pull caching model

anchors.1: Could not identify any trade-offs in the design
anchors.2: Mentioned one trade-off without deeper analysis
anchors.3: Discussed 2-3 trade-offs with pros/cons covering streaming, transcoding, or CDN
anchors.4: Systematically evaluated codec, protocol, caching, and cost trade-offs with bandwidth and latency modeling

commonMistakes: Serving raw uploaded files without transcoding to multiple resolutions
Not using adaptive bitrate streaming, forcing all users to same quality
Synchronous transcoding that blocks upload completion
Ignoring CDN and serving all video from origin servers
Not segmenting videos, requiring full download before playback
Underestimating the compute cost of transcoding at scale
